questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution requires every new long-term public program to include a funding plan. The plan must show whether the program will be paid for with ongoing revenue, temporary grants, or borrowed money. Supporters say this helps lawmakers avoid starting programs that cannot be maintained after the first year.
Which civic purpose is most directly supported by this requirement?

choices.A: Helping lawmakers connect long-term programs to reliable funding sources
choices.B: Giving private donors authority to pass state budgets
choices.C: Allowing courts to collect taxes without legislation
choices.D: Preventing the state from creating any public service

questions.correctAnswerText: Helping lawmakers connect long-term programs to reliable funding sources
questions.explanation: The requirement connects ongoing public programs to ongoing funding sources instead of temporary money alone.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A city compares two options for replacing buses.
Option: Diesel buses, Electric buses
Purchase cost per bus: $480,000, $720,000
Estimated annual fuel/energy cost per bus: $62,000, $24,000
Estimated annual maintenance cost per bus: $28,000, $18,000
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Electric buses cost more to buy but have lower estimated yearly operating costs.
choices.B: Diesel buses cost more to buy and have lower yearly operating costs.
choices.C: Both bus types have the same purchase cost.
choices.D: Electric buses have higher fuel/energy and maintenance costs.

questions.correctAnswerText: Electric buses cost more to buy but have lower estimated yearly operating costs.
questions.explanation: Electric buses have a higher purchase cost but lower fuel/energy and maintenance costs.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
In the early twentieth century, some Black women’s organizations campaigned for anti-lynching laws, better schools, voting rights, and public health services. Members wrote letters, organized local chapters, raised funds, and worked with newspapers to bring attention to injustice.
Which conclusion is best supported by the source?

choices.A: Black women’s organizations connected community service with political advocacy.
choices.B: Black women’s organizations avoided public issues.
choices.C: Anti-lynching campaigns were unrelated to civic participation.
choices.D: Public health and education were never part of reform movements.

questions.correctAnswerText: Black women’s organizations connected community service with political advocacy.
questions.explanation: The source describes campaigns for schools, public health, voting rights, and anti-lynching laws.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A described map shows three regions of a country. The western region has copper mines and rail links to a port. The central region has wheat farms and grain storage facilities. The eastern region has universities, hospitals, and software firms clustered near a major airport.
Which statement best explains the pattern shown on the described map?

choices.A: Natural resources, agriculture, and service industries are shaped by regional conditions and transportation links.
choices.B: All regions must depend on the same industry.
choices.C: Airports prevent service industries from developing.
choices.D: Wheat farms are unrelated to storage and transportation.

questions.correctAnswerText: Natural resources, agriculture, and service industries are shaped by regional conditions and transportation links.
questions.explanation: Mining, farming, and software clusters match different resources and transportation access.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A state creates a formula for distributing public library aid. The formula considers population served, distance to the nearest library branch, percentage of households without home internet, and local tax capacity.
Which TWO statements best explain the civic issue in this situation?

choices.A: Public funding formulas can consider both population size and barriers to access.
choices.B: Local tax capacity may affect how much state assistance a community needs.
choices.C: Library aid formulas are unrelated to public services.
choices.D: Communities without home internet must be excluded from public funding.
choices.E: Distance to services can never be considered in public policy.

questions.correctAnswerText: Public funding formulas can consider both population size and barriers to access. | Local tax capacity may affect how much state assistance a community needs.
questions.explanation: The formula weighs population, service distance, home internet access, and local ability to fund libraries.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Paired sources:
Source A:
A federal official argues that national standards are needed for digital medical records because patients often receive care in more than one state. The official says a common format can help hospitals, clinics, and emergency rooms share accurate information safely.
Source B:
A state health official argues that states should be allowed to require additional privacy safeguards for medical records. The official says state laws can respond to local concerns about data breaches, insurance discrimination, and patient trust.
What is the main disagreement between the two sources?

choices.A: Whether digital medical record rules should be nationally uniform or allow stronger state safeguards
choices.B: Whether hospitals should stop keeping medical records
choices.C: Whether states may appoint federal health officials
choices.D: Whether patients should be prevented from receiving care in other states

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether digital medical record rules should be nationally uniform or allow stronger state safeguards
questions.explanation: Source A favors national uniformity, while Source B supports stronger state privacy protections.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
During the Great Depression, some Mexican American families in the United States were pressured or forced to leave for Mexico, even when some family members were U.S. citizens. Local officials argued that removal would reduce welfare costs and open jobs. Critics later argued that these actions violated rights and separated families.
Which historical issue is most clearly reflected in the source?

choices.A: Economic crisis contributed to discriminatory removal policies and rights concerns.
choices.B: The Great Depression ended all migration between the United States and Mexico.
choices.C: Local officials had no role in Depression-era policy.
choices.D: Mexican American families were unaffected by unemployment and public relief debates.

questions.correctAnswerText: Economic crisis contributed to discriminatory removal policies and rights concerns.
questions.explanation: The source connects Depression-era economic pressures with removals that raised rights and family-separation concerns.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: When government power is divided between national and state governments, the system is called [D1]. When both levels may act in the same policy area, such as transportation or public safety, the powers may be [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=federalism; D2=concurrent
questions.explanation: Federalism divides authority between national and state governments, and concurrent powers may be shared.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A credit union offers a one-year savings certificate with simple interest of 3.5%. If a saver deposits $8,000, how many dollars of interest will the saver earn after one year?
Type your answer as a dollar amount without the dollar sign.
questions.explanation: $8,000 × 0.035 × 1 = $280 in simple interest.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A coastal region has expanded seafood processing, refrigerated warehouses, and export shipping. When ocean temperatures change fish migration patterns, some boats must travel farther, fuel costs rise, and processors worry about irregular supply.
Which conclusion is best supported by this scenario?

choices.A: Environmental change can affect resource industries and related trade networks.
choices.B: Fish migration patterns have no effect on coastal economies.
choices.C: Refrigerated warehouses eliminate all supply problems.
choices.D: Export shipping is unrelated to natural resources.

questions.correctAnswerText: Environmental change can affect resource industries and related trade networks.
questions.explanation: Changes in fish migration affect boats, fuel costs, processors, and exports.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A state law requires cities to update building codes for extreme heat, including shade requirements for certain public spaces and cooling standards for new senior housing. Cities may choose the exact design standards if they meet the state’s minimum safety goals.
Which statement best explains this state-local relationship?

choices.A: The state sets minimum public-safety goals while cities retain some implementation choices.
choices.B: Cities may ignore state building-safety requirements.
choices.C: Building codes are controlled only by private landlords.
choices.D: State safety goals prevent cities from making any local decisions.

questions.correctAnswerText: The state sets minimum public-safety goals while cities retain some implementation choices.
questions.explanation: The state sets heat-related safety requirements, while cities choose design details that meet them.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A table shows participation in neighborhood legal-aid clinics.
Year: 1965, 1985, 2005
Housing cases: 3,200, 8,900, 15,600
Immigration cases: 900, 4,800, 13,200
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Both types of cases increased, and immigration cases grew by a much larger percentage.
choices.B: Housing cases decreased after 1965.
choices.C: Immigration cases were higher than housing cases in every year.
choices.D: Both types of cases stayed the same from 1965 to 2005.

questions.correctAnswerText: Both types of cases increased, and immigration cases grew by a much larger percentage.
questions.explanation: Housing cases increased from 3,200 to 15,600, while immigration cases increased from 900 to 13,200, a much larger percentage increase.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution allows the legislature to create an ombudsman office for residents who have complaints about long-term care facilities. The office may investigate complaints, recommend corrective action, and report patterns of problems to lawmakers, but it cannot sentence anyone to criminal punishment.
Which statement best describes the role of the ombudsman office?

choices.A: It helps review complaints and identify service problems without acting as a criminal court.
choices.B: It replaces the legislature and courts in all health policy.
choices.C: It allows private facilities to write criminal penalties.
choices.D: It prevents residents from reporting care concerns.

questions.correctAnswerText: It helps review complaints and identify service problems without acting as a criminal court.
questions.explanation: The ombudsman investigates complaints and recommends changes but does not impose criminal punishment.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A country offers a temporary tax rebate for companies that build semiconductor factories domestically. Supporters say the policy could reduce dependence on foreign suppliers for important components. Critics argue that the rebate may cost the government revenue and benefit only selected firms.
Which conclusion is best supported by this scenario?

choices.A: Tax policy can be used to encourage domestic production while creating fiscal and fairness debates.
choices.B: Tax rebates cannot affect business investment decisions.
choices.C: Domestic production is unrelated to supply chains.
choices.D: Government revenue always increases when tax rebates expand.

questions.correctAnswerText: Tax policy can be used to encourage domestic production while creating fiscal and fairness debates.
questions.explanation: The rebate may encourage semiconductor production but can reduce revenue and raise fairness concerns.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution requires public disaster contracts to use written selection criteria, such as price, emergency response time, equipment availability, and past performance. Officials may not choose a contractor only because of a personal or political connection.
Which civic principle is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Public contracts should be awarded through neutral and job-related standards.
choices.B: Emergency contracts should be based on personal favoritism.
choices.C: Past performance can never be considered in public decisions.
choices.D: Written criteria are unnecessary for public spending.

questions.correctAnswerText: Public contracts should be awarded through neutral and job-related standards.
questions.explanation: Written criteria such as price, response time, equipment, and performance reduce favoritism.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
In the 1970s and 1980s, some women’s health organizations opened clinics, published guides, trained community educators, and advocated for changes in workplace and insurance policies. They argued that medical information and access to care were connected to equality in daily life.
Which TWO conclusions are best supported by this description?

choices.A: Women’s health activism connected public information, services, and policy change.
choices.B: Community education was one strategy used by health reform groups.
choices.C: Women’s health organizations focused only on foreign policy.
choices.D: Insurance and workplace policies were unrelated to access to care.
choices.E: Publishing guides prevented people from learning about health issues.

questions.correctAnswerText: Women’s health activism connected public information, services, and policy change. | Community education was one strategy used by health reform groups.
questions.explanation: The source describes clinics, guides, educators, and advocacy for workplace and insurance policy changes.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A region compares three transportation corridors.
Corridor: North, Central, South
Freight volume: 22 million tons, 34 million tons, 18 million tons
Average delay per shipment: 6 hours, 11 hours, 4 hours
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The Central corridor carries the most freight and has the longest average delay.
choices.B: The South corridor carries the most freight and has the longest delay.
choices.C: The North corridor has the lowest freight volume and the shortest delay.
choices.D: All corridors carry the same freight volume.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Central corridor carries the most freight and has the longest average delay.
questions.explanation: The Central corridor has the highest freight volume, 34 million tons, and the longest delay, 11 hours.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A court reviews a state grant program that gives extra funds to hospitals serving isolated mountain communities. The state argues that these hospitals face high transportation and staffing costs. Some urban hospitals argue that they serve more patients and also need support.
Which question is the court most likely considering?

choices.A: Whether the funding classification is reasonably connected to a public need
choices.B: Whether hospitals may declare foreign policy
choices.C: Whether grant programs must exclude rural communities
choices.D: Whether courts must operate hospitals directly

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether the funding classification is reasonably connected to a public need
questions.explanation: The court is weighing whether extra support for isolated hospitals is reasonably related to transportation and staffing costs.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A county compares two childcare support programs.
Program: Direct childcare vouchers, Employer childcare tax credit
Public cost per family served: $3,600, $2,400
Families served: 2,000, 1,200
Average family savings: $4,200, $2,100
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Direct vouchers cost more per family but serve more families and produce higher average family savings.
choices.B: Employer tax credits serve more families and have higher average savings.
choices.C: Both programs have the same cost per family.
choices.D: Direct vouchers serve fewer families than employer tax credits.

questions.correctAnswerText: Direct vouchers cost more per family but serve more families and produce higher average family savings.
questions.explanation: Direct vouchers cost $3,600 per family, serve 2,000 families, and save families an average of $4,200.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: During the late twentieth century, the growth of disability-access design in transportation, schools, and workplaces reflected efforts to expand [D1] and reduce [D2].
questions.correct: D1=participation in public life; D2=physical barriers
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=participation in public life; D2=physical barriers
questions.explanation: Disability-access design reduces physical barriers and expands participation in transportation, schools, and workplaces.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A state has $200 million available for a housing program. It commits $72 million to rental assistance, $48 million to housing repairs, and $35 million to shelter operations.
How much money remains uncommitted?

choices.A: $45 million
choices.B: $72 million
choices.C: $155 million
choices.D: $200 million

questions.correctAnswerText: $45 million
questions.explanation: $72 million + $48 million + $35 million = $155 million committed; $200 million − $155 million = $45 million.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state requires local governments to maintain public records in digital formats that can be transferred if a city office floods, burns, or loses power. Local governments may choose the software system, but the system must meet state standards for backup and access.
Which statement best describes this state-local relationship?

choices.A: The state requires a capability while allowing local governments to choose approved tools.
choices.B: Local governments may ignore record-preservation standards.
choices.C: Digital records are controlled only by private companies.
choices.D: State standards prevent local governments from keeping any records.

questions.correctAnswerText: The state requires a capability while allowing local governments to choose approved tools.
questions.explanation: Local governments must meet backup and access standards but may choose the software system.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A new border inspection system reduces average truck wait time from 9.5 hours to 6.25 hours. By how many hours did the average wait time decrease?
Type your answer as a decimal rounded to the nearest hundredth.
questions.explanation: 9.5 hours minus 6.25 hours equals 3.25 hours.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
In the late twentieth century, many Arab American communities developed cultural centers, business associations, student groups, newspapers, and civil rights organizations. These institutions helped communities respond to stereotypes, preserve cultural ties, and participate in public debates about immigration and foreign policy.
Which broader historical pattern is shown by the source?

choices.A: Community institutions supported cultural identity and civic participation.
choices.B: Arab American communities avoided public debate.
choices.C: Cultural centers prevented people from maintaining traditions.
choices.D: Immigration and foreign policy debates had no connection to community organizing.

questions.correctAnswerText: Community institutions supported cultural identity and civic participation.
questions.explanation: Cultural centers, associations, student groups, newspapers, and civil rights organizations supported identity and public participation.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

targets.T1: A system dividing power between national and state governments
targets.T2: Written standards used to award public contracts fairly
targets.T3: A public office that reviews complaints and recommends corrections

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Federalism; T2=Neutral selection criteria; T3=Ombudsman office
questions.explanation: Federalism divides national and state power, neutral criteria guide fair contract awards, and an ombudsman reviews complaints.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A bookstore imports many of its books from overseas printers. When shipping rates increase, the store orders more books from domestic printers, even though some titles cost slightly more. The owner says the change reduces delivery delays during the school season.
Which economic idea is most clearly shown?

choices.A: Businesses may trade lower shipping risk for higher purchase costs.
choices.B: Domestic suppliers always cost less than overseas suppliers.
choices.C: Shipping rates have no effect on sourcing decisions.
choices.D: Delivery delays cannot affect business choices.

questions.correctAnswerText: Businesses may trade lower shipping risk for higher purchase costs.
questions.explanation: The bookstore accepts slightly higher domestic printing costs to reduce delivery delays.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: When public money is legally reserved for one purpose, it is often called [D1]. If lawmakers want to use it for another purpose, they may need [D2].

D2: legal authorization or a rule change

questions.correct: D1=dedicated revenue; D2=legal authorization or a rule change
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=dedicated revenue; D2=legal authorization or a rule change
questions.explanation: Dedicated revenue is reserved for a purpose, and changing that use may require legal authorization or a rule change.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A court reviews a county charge on new warehouse developments. The county says the charge pays for road widening because warehouses add truck traffic. Developers argue that the charge should be based on each project’s expected traffic impact.
Which conclusion is best supported by the dispute?

choices.A: Development charges may need a reasonable connection to infrastructure costs created by the project.
choices.B: Counties may charge any amount without relation to public costs.
choices.C: Road widening cannot be funded through local charges.
choices.D: Courts cannot review development-related fees.

questions.correctAnswerText: Development charges may need a reasonable connection to infrastructure costs created by the project.
questions.explanation: The charge is defended as paying for road widening caused by warehouse truck traffic.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A table shows participation in language-preservation programs.
Year: 1995, 2010, 2025
Youth classes: 1,800, 4,600, 9,400
Adult classes: 2,200, 3,900, 6,100
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Youth class participation grew more than adult class participation.
choices.B: Adult class participation decreased after 1995.
choices.C: Youth classes had more participants than adult classes in 1995.
choices.D: Both groups had the same participation in 2025.

questions.correctAnswerText: Youth class participation grew more than adult class participation.
questions.explanation: Youth classes grew by 7,600 participants, while adult classes grew by 3,900.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A public technology fund has $84 million. It awards $26 million for school broadband, $18 million for cybersecurity, and $21 million for digital record systems.
How much money remains in the fund?

choices.A: $19 million
choices.B: $21 million
choices.C: $65 million
choices.D: $84 million

questions.correctAnswerText: $19 million
questions.explanation: Awards total $65 million; $84 million − $65 million = $19 million.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
Four neighboring towns form a shared animal-control service. Each town keeps its own local ordinances about licensing, but the shared service handles shelter space, emergency calls, and veterinary contracts. Costs are divided based on population and the number of calls from each town.
Which civic purpose is most directly supported by this arrangement?

choices.A: Sharing service capacity while preserving local rulemaking authority
choices.B: Allowing one town to cancel the ordinances of all others
choices.C: Replacing veterinary services with state courts
choices.D: Preventing towns from cooperating on local services

questions.correctAnswerText: Sharing service capacity while preserving local rulemaking authority
questions.explanation: The towns share shelter, calls, and contracts while keeping their own licensing ordinances.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A state compares applications for courthouse accessibility grants.
County: Ash, Brook, Stone
Entrances without ramps: 4, 2, 5
Annual visitors: 18,000, 26,000, 12,000
Local matching funds: $600,000, $900,000, $300,000
Which TWO statements are supported by the data?

choices.A: Stone County has the most entrances without ramps.
choices.B: Brook County has the highest annual visitors.
choices.C: Ash County has the lowest local matching funds.
choices.D: Brook County has more entrances without ramps than Stone County.
choices.E: Stone County has the highest local matching funds.

questions.correctAnswerText: Stone County has the most entrances without ramps. | Brook County has the highest annual visitors.
questions.explanation: Stone has 5 entrances without ramps, and Brook has the highest annual visitors at 26,000.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A described map shows a dry inland region with wind farms, a new transmission line, and factories that make parts for electric vehicles. Small towns along the transmission route have added training programs for electricians and maintenance workers.
Which conclusion is best supported by the described map?

choices.A: Energy infrastructure can influence manufacturing, workforce training, and regional development.
choices.B: Wind farms prevent towns from developing job-training programs.
choices.C: Transmission lines have no relationship to economic activity.
choices.D: Electric vehicle factories can operate only in coastal regions.

questions.correctAnswerText: Energy infrastructure can influence manufacturing, workforce training, and regional development.
questions.explanation: Wind farms, transmission lines, factories, and training programs are connected in the region.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A regional transit agency compares three service changes.
Change: Express bus route, Weekend rail service, Park-and-ride lot
Annual cost: $6 million, $9 million, $4 million
Estimated new riders per year: 900,000, 1,000,000, 350,000
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Weekend rail service costs the most and is expected to add the most riders.
choices.B: Park-and-ride lot costs the most and adds the most riders.
choices.C: Express bus route and weekend rail service have the same annual cost.
choices.D: Park-and-ride lot is expected to add more riders than weekend rail service.

questions.correctAnswerText: Weekend rail service costs the most and is expected to add the most riders.
questions.explanation: Weekend rail service costs $9 million and is expected to add 1,000,000 riders, both the highest values listed.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution requires that penalties collected from companies that violate data-security rules be used for cybersecurity training, identity-theft assistance, or technology upgrades for public agencies. Lawmakers may not transfer the money to unrelated programs unless the constitutional rule is changed.
Which claim is best supported by the source?

choices.A: Penalty revenue can be dedicated to a related public purpose.
choices.B: Data-security penalties must be used only for private gifts.
choices.C: Cybersecurity training cannot be funded by public money.
choices.D: Constitutional rules cannot restrict how penalty money is spent.

questions.correctAnswerText: Penalty revenue can be dedicated to a related public purpose.
questions.explanation: Data-security penalties are dedicated to cybersecurity training, identity-theft assistance, or public technology upgrades.
